<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no" ?>
<SnowReport>
	<_LastUpdated>2026-07-22T08:39:42</_LastUpdated>
	<TwentyFourHourSnowfall>0</TwentyFourHourSnowfall>
	<CurrentSnowdepth>40</CurrentSnowdepth>
	<AvSnowdepthinSnowmakingAreas>107</AvSnowdepthinSnowmakingAreas>
	<AvSnowdepthLastMeasuredOn>20/07/2026</AvSnowdepthLastMeasuredOn>
	<DateofLastSnowfall>14/07/2026</DateofLastSnowfall>

	<SeasonToDateSnowfall>96</SeasonToDateSnowfall>
	<Snowmaking>In Progress</Snowmaking>
	<WeatherConditionsasat6AM></WeatherConditionsasat6AM>
	<Temperature>0</Temperature>
	<OvernightMin>0</OvernightMin>
	<ForecastMax>2</ForecastMax>
	<Wind>Moderate/Strong 41-60 km/h</Wind>
	<WindDirection>NNW</WindDirection>
	<Precipitation>None</Precipitation>
	<ExpectedVisibility>Fair</ExpectedVisibility>
	<ExpectedCloudCover>Sunny</ExpectedCloudCover>
	<Forecast>Cloudy. Very high chance of snow showers, most likely from the late morning. Winds northwesterly 35 to 45 km/h tending westerly 30 to 40 km/h in the evening.Mostly sunny. Winds north to northwesterly 25 to 35 km/h increasing to 30 to 40 km/h in the late evening.</Forecast>
	<ResortReportHeadline>Mountain weather incoming!</ResortReportHeadline>
	<ResortReport><p>After an extended spell of amazingly sunny and warm July weather, it looks like we have some real mountain weather moving in.&nbsp; The wind has picked up, the temp has dropped and the clouds have rolled in.&nbsp; It's not snowing yet but we should see some flakes falling sometime today, continuing through tomorrow, and then we should have good snowmaking conditions for the next few days.&nbsp; &nbsp;We are on the way up!</p><p>The snowmakers had another good run overnight, so the groomers should be in good shape for carving turns again this morning. Off piste areas will be best avoided until we have had precipitation of any type.</p><p>Heavenly Valley area is open but the cover is thin, the snow is hard and the runs are only suitable for advanced and expert skiers and riders.&nbsp; The extreme area is closed due to a significant ice hazard, and please do not attempt to retrieve dropped equipment from closed areas of the resort.</p><p>Rip up the groomers to start with and see how the day pans out before getting adventurous.&nbsp; Enjoy</p><p>Cheers Bill</p></ResortReport>
	<HideReportEDMWeb>False</HideReportEDMWeb>
	<LiftsOpen></LiftsOpen>
	<LiftsStandby></LiftsStandby>
	<LiftsClosed></LiftsClosed>
	<RunsOpen></RunsOpen>
	<RunsGroomed></RunsGroomed>
	<RunsSnowmaking>5</RunsSnowmaking>
	<TerrainParks></TerrainParks>
	<Backcountry>22 July 2026

There is a significant sliding hazard in the backcountry again today, plus the cover is thin, becoming more marginal the lower you go. All aspects will likely stay very icy and dangerous for the day.  Take Care. </Backcountry>
	<Avalanche></Avalanche>
	<HeliLink>Closed</HeliLink>
</SnowReport>
